USA: Enron's bankruptcy filing could trigger a wave of buybacks.
By Peter Eavis

10/27/2001
Reuters English News Service
(C) Reuters Limited 2001.

NEW YORK, Oct 27 (Reuters) - Enron Corp's bankruptcy filing could trigger a wave of buybacks by energy companies, analysts said on Monday. 
"It's a possibility that we could see a lot of companies that have been sitting on the sidelines, that have been holding back on their buybacks, now deciding to do so," said John Kinser, an analyst at Prudential Securities in New York. 
Enron, the largest energy trader, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on Monday, saying it had lost $1.2 billion since mid-August in trading energy and other commodities. 
The company's shares fell $1.50 to $17.50 in trading on the New York Stock Exchange.

Reuters English News Service

NEW YORK, Oct 27 (Reuters) - Shares of Enron Corp. fell $1.50 to $17.50 in heavy trading on Wall Street on Monday, after the energy trader's bankruptcy filing triggered a wave of buybacks by energy companies. 
"It's a buyback tsunami," said one trader. 
Enron's shares fell $1.50 to $17.50 in heavy trading on the New York Stock Exchange, after the Houston-based company said it would seek Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. 
The company's shares have fallen more than 80 percent since Oct. 16, when it disclosed that it had lost $1.2 billion on questionable transactions with partnerships run by its former chief financial officer, Andrew Fastow.
